h68a5b8c7g8414g4f45g8a1ag0788b2d45a65: Safe? No where's safe! I can't even close my eyes without -

h65b31d40g24e6g4338g904egca0dc31899da: So tell me, what do you make of my dream?

h12076ac3ge1adg4362g9f34g47407e5802fa: He used to carve his verses into our flesh. Said he'd turn us into art.
hf4d2a5a4gc7eeg47dbga834gca0ccff5daf9: He once spent all night with a razor, drafting and redrafting a sonnet on my back.

h209f0856gb246g417cg8478gd3535989e394: I think I can guess what you two are talking about - the dreams, yes? The visitor?
he1ed1208gf3abg48cega31eg44dd41e84bc6: This began when we started using our infection to influence the minds of others. I don't think that's a coincidence.
h69785434g5db2g43ddgba11ge24e1c6b996e: I hear you had dreams too? The brain-bugs got busy last night. And I've got a theory.
h2b4875bbgcc3bg40bagb56eg1cbf5855d12d: When you call on the tadpole to influence others, we pay the price that very night. All of us.

h2529600fg8bf4g4117gbadfgcdfb7bbb1e58: Those nightmarish visions of yours, Astarion, they aren't dreams - they're projections. Illithid ones.
h31e1f961ge3aeg4880gb911g9c8836943ec9: The tadpoles give us the power to bend others' wills, but using that power clearly comes at a price. Are we willing to pay it?

hfe8a4530gdfaag43d7g9f5cg391ab21f35a3: Wait - the tadpole. Every time we've used its power to bend a mind, we've had these dreams.
